component_identification.content: Core components include island boards, invader board, fear deck, blight cards, major and minor power decks, spirit panels, power cards, presence tokens, Dahan pieces, invader pieces (explorers, towns, cities), and scenario/adversary panels. Each spirit has unique starting powers, growth options, innate powers, and presence tracks that unlock card plays and energy income.

core_game_loop.content: Each round proceeds through Spirit Phase, Fast Power Phase, Invader Phase, Slow Power Phase, and Time Passes. During Spirit Phase, players choose growth, gain energy, and select cards to play. Fast powers resolve before invaders act; invaders then ravage, build, and explore according to invader cards. Slow powers resolve afterward to set up the next turn. Fear generation can trigger fear cards and eventually a win condition.

detailed_rules.content: Invader actions are deterministic: ravage damages land first (to Dahan/Presence and potentially blighting), then surviving Dahan counterattack; build adds towns or cities in lands matching the current build card where invaders are present; explore adds explorers from coasts/towns/cities into the explore terrain. Blight cascades when excess damage hits already-blighted lands. Spirits place presence via growth, extending range and improving tracks. Card plays require energy and range/target legality. Innate powers activate when played card elements meet thresholds. Major powers are stronger but often require forgetting a card. Fear is generated mainly by destroying invaders and some power effects; each earned fear card is resolved at specific timing windows. Win condition steps down by terror level: destroy all cities at Terror II, all towns/cities at Terror III, and all invaders at Terror I baseline. Loss can occur if blight card conditions fail, the fear deck empties before victory, or presence is fully removed for a spirit in solo.

scoring_and_endgame.content: In standard play there is no point scoring; the team either wins or loses. Victory occurs when current terror-level condition is met and no pending loss condition interrupts resolution. Difficulty is tuned with adversaries and scenarios, and many groups track performance by difficulty level beaten rather than points.

beginner_strategy.content: Focus first on preventing ravages that would blight critical lands. Use Dahan counterattacks efficiently by pushing or gathering invaders into planned defense turns. Coordinate fast powers to interrupt build/explore chains before they snowball. Specialize roles: one spirit controls board position while another provides offense or defense. Do not overvalue immediate destruction if fear tempo and blight control are suffering; sustainable control wins longer games.
